segments.text: I am a rose of Sharon, a lily of the valleys.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: Like a lily among thorns is my darling among the young women.
segments.speaker: Beloved Man

segments.text: Like an apple tree among the trees of the forest is my beloved among the young men. I delight to sit in his shade, and his fruit is sweet to my taste.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: Let him lead me to the banquet hall, and let his banner over me be love.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: Strengthen me with raisins, refresh me with apples, for I am faint with love.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: His left arm is under my head, and his right arm embraces me.
Daughters of Jerusalem, I charge you by the gazelles and by the does of the field: Do not arouse or awaken love until it so desires.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: Listen! My beloved! Look! Here he comes, leaping across the mountains, bounding over the hills.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: My beloved is like a gazelle or a young stag. Look! There he stands behind our wall, gazing through the windows, peering through the lattice.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: My beloved spoke and said to me,
Arise, my darling, my beautiful one, come with me.
segments.speaker: Beloved Man

segments.text: See! The winter is past; the rains are over and gone.
segments.speaker: Beloved Man

segments.text: Flowers appear on the earth; the season of singing has come, the cooing of doves is heard in our land.
segments.speaker: Beloved Man

segments.text: The fig tree forms its early fruit; the blossoming vines spread their fragrance. Arise, come, my darling; my beautiful one, come with me.
segments.speaker: Beloved Man

segments.text: My dove in the clefts of the rock, in the hiding places on the mountainside, show me your face, let me hear your voice; for your voice is sweet, and your face is lovely.
segments.speaker: Beloved Man

segments.text: Catch for us the foxes, the little foxes that ruin the vineyards, our vineyards that are in bloom.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: My beloved is mine and I am his; he browses among the lilies.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman

segments.text: Until the day breaks and the shadows flee, turn, my beloved, and be like a gazelle or like a young stag on the rugged hills.
segments.speaker: Beloved Woman
